channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Electric Trucks Poised to Transform Australian Freight Amid Rising Fuel Costs
item.description: For years, electric trucks were considered impractical for long-distance freight, but recent advances in battery technology and charging infrastructure are changing that. In China, electric and hybrid...

item.title: Vioja Mahakamani Actor Sgt. Obadiah Passes Away, Family Seeks Support for Burial
item.description: The Kenyan entertainment industry is in shock after the passing of Joseph Githinji Mukaria, popularly known as Sgt. Obadiah or Mzae Comedy, a veteran actor from Vioja Mahakamani. He died on Sunday, Ma...

item.title: Germany and Japan Release Oil Reserves Amid Global Supply Concerns
item.description: Germany and Japan have announced plans to release portions of their strategic oil reserves in response to rising global energy concerns. This move comes as part of coordinated efforts by the G7 countr...

item.title: Bank of England announces UK wildlife theme for next series of banknotes, ending use of historical figures
item.description: The Bank of England has revealed that the next series of its banknotes will feature images of British wildlife rather than historical figures such as William Shakespeare, Sir Winston Churchill, Jane A...

item.title: Maritime Tensions Rise in Strait of Hormuz Amid US–Israel–Iran Conflict
item.description: On the twelfth day of the US–Israel conflict with Iran, the Strait of Hormuz has emerged as a key hotspot shaping the strategic direction of the war. In the last 24 hours alone, at least three separat...

item.title: 16-year-old girl injured in stabbing incident at Thorpe St Andrew School
item.description: On Wednesday morning, a teenage girl was stabbed at Thorpe St Andrew School, located near Norwich, prompting a swift response from emergency services. Police were called to the scene at 10:24 am follo...

item.title: Rising Diesel Costs Cause Losses for UK Fuel Retailers Amid Middle East Conflict
item.description: Fuel retailers in the UK are reportedly losing money on diesel sales following sharp increases in wholesale prices triggered by the ongoing Middle East conflict. The Petrol Retailers' Association (PRA...

item.title: How inclusive finance can narrow Kenya’s gender gap
item.description: This article looks at how inclusive finance can be used practically to reduce Kenya’s persistent gender gap in economic opportunities and outcomes. It explains that many Kenyan women still face seriou...

item.title: Air India Express Flight from Hyderabad Experiences Hard Landing at Phuket; No Injuries Reported
item.description: An Air India Express Boeing 737 MAX, carrying 138 people, made a hard landing at Phuket Airport on 11 March 2026 after departing from Hyderabad. The aircraft reportedly bounced on first touchdown, and...

item.title: Enrique Razon tops Forbes 2026 list as Manny Villar's net worth declines
item.description: According to Forbes' 40th annual World’s Billionaires list released in March 2026, Enrique Razon Jr., chairman of International Container Terminal Services Inc., is now the richest Filipino with a net...

item.title: Collection of Political Cartoons Published on 12 March 2026
item.description: This article presents a curated gallery of political cartoons published on 12 March 2026. It allows readers to view satirical illustrations highlighting current political events and social commentary ...

item.title: Strengthening BRICS-Africa Partnerships to Address Development and Climate Challenges in 2026
item.description: Africa just wrapped up its G20, but the results left many disappointed as real politics and global power struggles overshadowed meaningful progress. No concrete solutions came up for African debt, pea...

item.title: House approves bill granting President power to suspend or reduce fuel excise tax amid Middle East conflict
item.description: On March 11, 2026, the House of Representatives approved on second reading House Bill No. 8418, which gives President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. emergency powers to suspend or reduce the excise tax on fuel ...

item.title: South Africa Assured of Stable Fuel Supply Despite Refinery Maintenance
item.description: The Department of Mineral and Petroleum Resources (DMPR) has assured South Africans that there is no immediate risk of fuel shortages, despite ongoing concerns raised in the media. This reassurance co...

item.title: Majority of Americans Prefer No Tariffs on Canada Amid Trade Talks
item.description: A recent Angus Reid survey indicates that slightly over half of Americans oppose tariffs on Canada, reflecting a largely positive view of the northern neighbour despite ongoing trade tensions under fo...

item.title: Seven Iranian Women Footballers Defect in Australia Amid Fears of Repression
item.description: During the AFC Women's Asian Cup in Australia, Iran's women's football team refused to sing their national anthem before their opening match against South Korea, sparking outrage in Tehran where state...

item.title: Senate postpones deliberation on Independent People’s Commission bill until May or June
item.description: Senate President Vicente “Tito” Sotto III confirmed on March 11, 2026, that the Senate will not be able to pass the proposed Independent People’s Commission (IPC) bill before the upcoming session susp...

item.title: High Court Recognises Eldoret Woman as Legal Beneficiary of Late Politician’s Estate
item.description: Chepkoech Too, a young woman from Eldoret, has finally been recognised as a rightful heir to her late father’s estate after a long legal struggle that lasted nearly ten years. The late Mark Kiptarbei ...

item.title: PM Modi inaugurates over ₹10,800 crore infrastructure projects in Kochi after roadshow
item.description: Prime Minister Narendra Modi visited Kochi on 11 March 2026, holding a grand roadshow that drew large crowds of BJP supporters ahead of inaugurating development projects worth over ₹10,800 crore. The ...
